name: Template Documentation

tasks.title: Document template override mechanism in configuration.md
tasks.description: Document template override mechanism in configuration.md
tasks.body: Add a section to docs/humans/configuration.md explaining how users can override generated file templates. Cover: where default templates live (internal/project/templates/), how to override by placing .tmpl files in system/custom/templates/ or system/local/templates/, the three-tier resolution order (base < custom < local), available templates and their variables (ADR, spec, scaffold READMEs, gitignore, task markdown). Include an example showing how to customize the ADR format by adding a Supersedes section.
acceptance_criteria: Template override mechanism is documented with examples
All overridable templates are listed with their variables

tasks.title: Update CLI docs and CONTRIBUTING.md
tasks.body: Update docs/humans/cli/adr-create.md (if it exists, or the relevant CLI doc) to mention that the ADR output format is customizable via templates. Update spec-create docs similarly. Update CONTRIBUTING.md to add guidance in the 'Adding a CLI Command' section (or equivalent) about using templates for commands that generate files: use RenderToFile, create a .tmpl file under templates/, define a typed context struct, and add a snapshot test.
acceptance_criteria: CONTRIBUTING.md mentions templates for file-generating commands
CLI docs mention template customization where applicable

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Template Documentation is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify docs include concrete examples of template override, not just abstract descriptions

breadcrumbs.text: Added Template Overrides section to docs/humans/configuration.md. Covers artifact templates (ADR, spec, task, audit-task) with their tier paths and template variables, scaffold templates and why they aren't overridable, three-tier resolution order, and a worked example showing ADR customization with a Supersedes section.

Updated adr-create.md, spec-create.md, and CONTRIBUTING.md. CLI docs now have a Template Customization section linking to configuration.md. CONTRIBUTING.md explains the template workflow (tmpl file, context struct, RenderToFile, snapshot test) for file-generating commands.

Audit reviewed 4 files: docs/humans/configuration.md (Template Overrides section, lines 314-398), docs/humans/cli/adr-create.md (Template Customization section), docs/humans/cli/spec-create.md (Template Customization section), CONTRIBUTING.md (file-generating command guidance). PASS. Verified all template variable inventories against internal/pipeline/template_data.go structs — exact match for ADR, Spec, Task, and audit-task types. Verified PromptRepository.ResolveTemplate and RenderToFile references against repository.go signatures. All cross-references between CLI docs and configuration.md resolve correctly. One accepted finding: AuditTaskData struct has a NodeName field but docs say 'None (static content)' — accurate because the template is rendered with nil data (project_create.go:44). cmd test failure and gofmt violations are pre-existing, unrelated to this node's documentation-only changes.

audit.result_summary: Documented the template override mechanism across four files. configuration.md gained a Template Overrides subsection covering artifact templates (ADR, spec, task, audit-task) with their tier paths, Go template variables, resolution semantics, scaffold template limitations, and a worked ADR customization example. adr-create.md and spec-create.md each gained a Template Customization section linking back to the authoritative reference in configuration.md. CONTRIBUTING.md's 'Adding a CLI Command' section was extended with a four-step workflow for file-generating commands: create a .tmpl, define a typed context struct, render via RenderToFile, add a snapshot test. All variable inventories verified against source (template_data.go), all API references verified against implementation (repository.go), all cross-references resolve correctly.

task-0001.objective: Document the template override mechanism in configuration.md so users know how to customize generated artifacts.
task-0001.what_happened: Added a Template Overrides subsection under Common Customizations in docs/humans/configuration.md. Covers artifact templates (ADR, spec, task, audit-task) with exact tier paths and Go template variables, explains scaffold templates and why they are not overridable through tiers, and includes a worked example adding a Supersedes section to the ADR template. Verified all variable names against internal/pipeline/template_data.go and the actual .tmpl files.
went_well: Template variable inventory is accurate against source code, not guessed from task description
Distinguished artifact templates (overridable at runtime) from scaffold templates (one-time init, not tier-resolved) to prevent user confusion
improvements: The task description mentioned system/custom/templates/ as the override path, but actual tier paths have no templates/ subdirectory. Documented the real paths.
action_items: If a template list command is added later (wolfcastle template list), link to it from this section

task-0002.objective: Update CLI docs and CONTRIBUTING.md to document template customization for file-generating commands
task-0002.what_happened: Added a Template Customization section to both adr-create.md and spec-create.md CLI docs, each linking back to the Template Overrides section in configuration.md. Extended CONTRIBUTING.md's Adding a CLI Command section with four-step guidance for commands that generate files: create a .tmpl, define a typed context struct, render via RenderToFile, add a snapshot test.
went_well: Cross-referenced configuration.md rather than duplicating template variable tables, keeping a single source of truth
Verified template_data.go exists and RenderToFile signature before referencing them in docs
improvements: Could have also updated task-add.md since task add also uses the template system, but the task description only called out adr-create and spec-create
action_items: If task-add CLI docs are updated later, add a similar Template Customization section pointing to configuration.md
